pli-tv-kd1:73.4.8.eng: “If you notice that you are enjoying your stay in the wilderness, but unable to obtain formal support, I allow you to live without.

pli-tv-kd1:73.4.9.pli: yadā patirūpo nissayadāyako āgacchissati, tadā tassa nissāya vasissāmī”ti.
pli-tv-kd1:73.4.9.eng: When a suitable support-giver comes, you should live with formal support from him.”

pli-tv-kd1:74.1.0.eng: 60. The allowance to make proclamations using the family name

pli-tv-kd1:74.1.1.pli: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmato mahākassapassa upasampadāpekkho hoti.
pli-tv-kd1:74.1.1.eng: At one time a certain person wanted the full ordination with Venerable Mahākassapa.

pli-tv-kd1:74.1.2.pli: Atha kho āyasmā mahākassapo āyasmato ānandassa santike dūtaṁ pāhesi—
pli-tv-kd1:74.1.2.eng: Mahākassapa sent a message to Venerable Ānanda:

pli-tv-kd1:74.1.3.eng: “Please come, Ānanda, and do the proclamation.”

pli-tv-kd1:74.1.5.pli: “nāhaṁ ussahāmi therassa nāmaṁ gahetuṁ, garu me thero”ti.
pli-tv-kd1:74.1.5.eng: “Because I respect the elder so much, I can’t say his name.”

pli-tv-kd1:74.1.7.eng: “I allow you to do the proclamation also using the family name.”

pli-tv-kd1:74.2.0.eng: 61. The two people seeking the full ordination, etc.

pli-tv-kd1:74.2.1.pli: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmato mahākassapassa dve upasampadāpekkhā honti.
pli-tv-kd1:74.2.1.eng: At one time there were two people who wanted the full ordination with Venerable Mahākassapa.

pli-tv-kd1:74.2.3.eng: about who should be ordained first.

pli-tv-kd1:74.2.5.eng: “I allow you to give the full ordination to two people with a single proclamation.”

pli-tv-kd1:74.3.1.pli: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulānaṁ therānaṁ upasampadāpekkhā honti.
pli-tv-kd1:74.3.1.eng: At one time there were a number of people who wanted the full ordination with several senior monks.
pli-tv-kd1:74.3.1.comm: “Several” renders sambahula. See Appendix of Technical Terms.

pli-tv-kd1:74.3.3.eng: about who should be ordained first.

pli-tv-kd1:74.3.5.eng: “Well then, let’s ordain all of them with a single proclamation.”

pli-tv-kd1:74.3.7.pli: “Anujānāmi, bhikkhave, dve tayo ekānussāvane kātuṁ, tañca kho ekena upajjhāyena, na tveva nānupajjhāyenā”ti.
pli-tv-kd1:74.3.7.eng: “I allow you to give the full ordination to two or three people with a single proclamation, but only with a single preceptor, not with many.”

pli-tv-kd1:75.1.0.eng: 62. The allowance to be fully ordained when one is twenty years old since appearing in the womb

pli-tv-kd1:75.1.1.pli: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā kumārakassapo gabbhavīso upasampanno ahosi.
pli-tv-kd1:75.1.1.eng: At that time Venerable Kumārakassapa had been given the full ordination twenty years after he appeared in the womb.

pli-tv-kd1:75.1.3.eng: “The Buddha has laid down a rule

pli-tv-kd1:75.1.4.eng: that a person less than twenty years old shouldn’t be given the full ordination.

pli-tv-kd1:75.1.5.eng: I was ordained twenty years after appearing in the womb.

pli-tv-kd1:75.1.6.pli: Upasampanno nu khomhi, nanu kho upasampanno”ti?
pli-tv-kd1:75.1.6.eng: I wonder, have I been ordained or not?”

pli-tv-kd1:75.1.8.pli: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, mātukucchismiṁ paṭhamaṁ cittaṁ uppannaṁ, paṭhamaṁ viññāṇaṁ pātubhūtaṁ, tadupādāya sāvassa jāti.
pli-tv-kd1:75.1.8.eng: “When the mind first appears in the mother’s womb, when the consciousness first manifests, that’s a person’s birth.

pli-tv-kd1:75.1.9.eng: I allow you to give the full ordination to someone who is twenty years old since appearing in the womb.”

pli-tv-kd1:76.1.1.pli: Tena kho pana samayena upasampannā dissanti kuṭṭhikāpi gaṇḍikāpi kilāsikāpi sosikāpi apamārikāpi.
pli-tv-kd1:76.1.1.eng: At that time the full ordination had been given to people who had leprosy, abscesses, mild leprosy, tuberculosis, and epilepsy.
pli-tv-kd1:76.1.1.comm: For an explanation of the Pali terminology behind these illnesses, see Appendix of Medical Terminology.

pli-tv-kd1:76.1.3.pli: “Anujānāmi, bhikkhave, upasampādentena terasa antarāyike dhamme pucchituṁ.
pli-tv-kd1:76.1.3.eng: “The one who is giving the full ordination should ask about thirteen obstacles.

pli-tv-kd1:76.1.4.eng: It should be done like this:

pli-tv-kd1:76.1.5.eng: ‘Do you have any of these diseases:

pli-tv-kd1:76.1.6.eng: leprosy, abscesses, mild leprosy, tuberculosis, or epilepsy?

pli-tv-kd1:76.1.11.eng: Are you employed by the king?

pli-tv-kd1:76.1.12.eng: Do you have your parents’ permission?

pli-tv-kd1:76.1.14.eng: Do you have a full set of bowl and robes?

pli-tv-kd1:76.1.16.eng: What’s the name of your preceptor?’”

pli-tv-kd1:76.2.1.pli: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū ananusiṭṭhe upasampadāpekkhe antarāyike dhamme pucchanti.
pli-tv-kd1:76.2.1.eng: Soon afterwards they asked those seeking the full ordination about the obstacles without first instructing them.

pli-tv-kd1:76.2.2.pli: Upasampadāpekkhā vitthāyanti, maṅkū honti, na sakkonti vissajjetuṁ.
pli-tv-kd1:76.2.2.eng: They were embarrassed, humiliated, and unable to respond.

pli-tv-kd1:76.2.4.pli: “Anujānāmi, bhikkhave, paṭhamaṁ anusāsitvā pacchā antarāyike dhamme pucchitun”ti.
pli-tv-kd1:76.2.4.eng: “You should instruct first and then ask about the obstacles.

pli-tv-kd1:76.3.1.eng: They instructed them right there in the midst of the Sangha.

pli-tv-kd1:76.3.2.pli: Upasampadāpekkhā tatheva vitthāyanti, maṅkū honti, na sakkonti vissajjetuṁ.
pli-tv-kd1:76.3.2.eng: Once more those seeking the full ordination were embarrassed, humiliated, and unable to respond.

pli-tv-kd1:76.3.4.pli: “Anujānāmi, bhikkhave, ekamantaṁ anusāsitvā saṅghamajjhe antarāyike dhamme pucchituṁ.
pli-tv-kd1:76.3.4.eng: “You should instruct them at a distance and then ask about the obstacles in the midst of the Sangha.

pli-tv-kd1:76.3.5.eng: And it should be done like this.

pli-tv-kd1:76.3.6.eng: First they should be told to choose a preceptor.

pli-tv-kd1:76.3.7.eng: Their bowls and robes should then be pointed out to them:

pli-tv-kd1:76.3.8.pli: ‘ayaṁ te patto, ayaṁ saṅghāṭi, ayaṁ uttarāsaṅgo, ayaṁ antaravāsako.
pli-tv-kd1:76.3.8.eng: ‘This is your bowl, this your outer robe, this your upper robe, and this your sarong.

pli-tv-kd1:76.3.9.eng: Now please go and stand over there.’”

pli-tv-kd1:76.4.1.eng: Then they were instructed by monks who were ignorant and incompetent.

pli-tv-kd1:76.4.2.pli: Duranusiṭṭhā upasampadāpekkhā vitthāyanti, maṅkū honti, na sakkonti vissajjetuṁ.
pli-tv-kd1:76.4.2.eng: And because they were badly instructed, they were once again embarrassed, humiliated, and unable to respond.

pli-tv-kd1:76.4.4.eng: “A monk who is ignorant and incompetent shouldn’t instruct.

pli-tv-kd1:76.4.5.eng: If he does, he commits an offense of wrong conduct.

pli-tv-kd1:76.4.6.pli: Anujānāmi, bhikkhave, byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena anusāsitun”ti.
pli-tv-kd1:76.4.6.eng: A monk who is competent and capable should instruct.”

pli-tv-kd1:76.5.1.eng: They instructed without having been appointed.

pli-tv-kd1:76.5.3.eng: “A monk shouldn’t instruct if he hasn’t been appointed.

pli-tv-kd1:76.5.4.eng: If he does, he commits an offense of wrong conduct.

pli-tv-kd1:76.5.5.eng: I allow a monk to instruct if he’s been appointed to do so.

pli-tv-kd1:76.5.6.eng: And it should be done like this.

pli-tv-kd1:76.5.7.pli: attanā vā attānaṁ sammannitabbaṁ, parena vā paro sammannitabbo.
pli-tv-kd1:76.5.7.eng: One is either appointed through oneself or through someone else.

pli-tv-kd1:76.5.8.eng: How is one appointed through oneself?

pli-tv-kd1:76.5.9.eng: A competent and capable monk should inform the Sangha:

pli-tv-kd1:76.5.10.eng: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd1:76.5.11.eng: So-and-so is seeking the full ordination with venerable so-and-so.

pli-tv-kd1:76.5.12.pli: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, ahaṁ itthannāmaṁ anusāseyyan’ti.
pli-tv-kd1:76.5.12.eng: If the Sangha is ready, I will instruct so-and-so.’

pli-tv-kd1:76.6.1.eng: And how is one appointed through someone else?

pli-tv-kd1:76.6.2.eng: A competent and capable monk should inform the Sangha:

pli-tv-kd1:76.6.3.eng: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd1:76.6.4.eng: So-and-so is seeking the full ordination with venerable so-and-so.

pli-tv-kd1:76.6.5.pli: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, itthannāmo itthannāmaṁ anusāseyyā’ti.
pli-tv-kd1:76.6.5.eng: If the Sangha is ready, so-and-so will instruct so-and-so.’

pli-tv-kd1:76.7.1.pli: Tena sammatena bhikkhunā upasampadāpekkho upasaṅkamitvā evamassa vacanīyo—
pli-tv-kd1:76.7.1.eng: The appointed monk should go to the one who is seeking the full ordination and say this:

pli-tv-kd1:76.7.2.eng: ‘Listen, so-and-so. Now is the time for you to tell the truth.

pli-tv-kd1:76.7.3.pli: Yaṁ jātaṁ taṁ saṅghamajjhe pucchante santaṁ atthīti vattabbaṁ, asantaṁ natthīti vattabbaṁ.
pli-tv-kd1:76.7.3.eng: You will be asked in the midst of the Sangha about various matters. If something is true, you should say, “Yes,” and if it’s not, you should say, “No.”

pli-tv-kd1:76.7.4.pli: Mā kho vitthāyi, mā kho maṅku ahosi.

pli-tv-kd1:76.7.5.eng: This is what they’ll ask you:

pli-tv-kd1:76.7.6.eng: “Do you have any of these diseases:

pli-tv-kd1:76.7.7.eng: leprosy, abscesses, mild leprosy, tuberculosis, or epilepsy?

pli-tv-kd1:76.7.12.eng: Are you employed by the king?

pli-tv-kd1:76.7.13.eng: Do you have your parents’ permission?

pli-tv-kd1:76.7.15.eng: Do you have a full set of bowl and robes?

pli-tv-kd1:76.7.17.eng: What’s the name of your preceptor?”’”

pli-tv-kd1:76.8.1.eng: They then returned to the Sangha together.

pli-tv-kd1:76.8.2.eng: The Buddha said, “They shouldn’t return together.

pli-tv-kd1:76.8.3.eng: The instructor should come first and inform the Sangha:

pli-tv-kd1:76.8.4.eng: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd1:76.8.5.eng: So-and-so is seeking the full ordination with venerable so-and-so.

pli-tv-kd1:76.8.7.eng: If the Sangha is ready, so-and-so should come.’

pli-tv-kd1:76.8.8.eng: And he should be told to come.

pli-tv-kd1:76.8.9.pli: Ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ kārāpetvā bhikkhūnaṁ pāde vandāpetvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīdāpetvā añjaliṁ paggaṇhāpetvā upasampadaṁ yācāpetabbo—
pli-tv-kd1:76.8.9.eng: He should then arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, pay respect at the feet of the monks, squat on his heels, and raise his joined palms. He should then ask for the full ordination:

pli-tv-kd1:76.8.10.eng: ‘Venerables, I ask the Sangha for the full ordination.

pli-tv-kd1:76.8.11.pli: Ullumpatu maṁ, bhante, saṅgho anukampaṁ upādāya.
pli-tv-kd1:76.8.11.eng: Please lift me up out of compassion.

pli-tv-kd1:76.8.12.eng: For the second time, venerables, I ask the Sangha for the full ordination.

pli-tv-kd1:76.8.13.pli: Ullumpatu maṁ, bhante, saṅgho anukampaṁ upādāya.
pli-tv-kd1:76.8.13.eng: Please lift me up out of compassion.

pli-tv-kd1:76.8.14.eng: For the third time, venerables, I ask the Sangha for the full ordination.

pli-tv-kd1:76.8.15.pli: Ullumpatu maṁ, bhante, saṅgho anukampaṁ upādāyā’ti.
pli-tv-kd1:76.8.15.eng: Please lift me up out of compassion.’

pli-tv-kd1:76.9.1.eng: A competent and capable monk should then inform the Sangha:

pli-tv-kd1:76.9.2.eng: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd1:76.9.3.eng: So-and-so is seeking the full ordination with venerable so-and-so.

pli-tv-kd1:76.9.4.pli: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, ahaṁ itthannāmaṁ antarāyike dhamme puccheyyanti?
pli-tv-kd1:76.9.4.eng: If the Sangha is ready, I will ask so-and-so about the obstacles.

pli-tv-kd1:76.9.5.pli: Suṇasi, itthannāma, ayaṁ te saccakālo bhūtakālo.
pli-tv-kd1:76.9.5.eng: Listen, so-and-so. Now is the time for you to tell the truth.

pli-tv-kd1:76.9.6.eng: I will ask you about various matters.

pli-tv-kd1:76.9.7.pli: Santaṁ atthīti vattabbaṁ, asantaṁ natthīti vattabbaṁ.
pli-tv-kd1:76.9.7.eng: If something is true, you should say, “Yes,” and if it’s not, you should say, “No.”

pli-tv-kd1:76.9.8.eng: Do you have any of these diseases:

pli-tv-kd1:76.9.9.eng: leprosy, abscesses, mild leprosy, tuberculosis, or epilepsy?

pli-tv-kd1:76.9.14.eng: Are you employed by the king?

pli-tv-kd1:76.9.15.eng: Do you have your parents’ permission?

pli-tv-kd1:76.9.17.eng: Do you have a full set of bowl and robes?

pli-tv-kd1:76.9.19.eng: What’s the name of your preceptor?’

pli-tv-kd1:76.10.1.eng: A competent and capable monk should then inform the Sangha:

pli-tv-kd1:76.10.2.eng: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd1:76.10.3.pli: Ayaṁ itthannāmo itthannāmassa āyasmato upasampadāpekkho, parisuddho antarāyikehi dhammehi, paripuṇṇassa pattacīvaraṁ.
pli-tv-kd1:76.10.3.eng: So-and-so is seeking the full ordination with venerable so-and-so. He is free from obstacles and his bowl and robes are complete.
pli-tv-kd1:76.10.3.comm: The Pali reads: Ayaṁ itthannāmo itthannāmassa āyasmato upasampadāpekkho. Taking the genitive case here to be the agent genitive, which seems to be the most obvious reading, this would mean, “So-and-so who is seeking to be fully ordained by venerable so-and-so.” But it is the Sangha that ordains, not individuals, and so this translation does not seem quite right. Vmv 3.126: Ayaṁ buddharakkhito āyasmato dhammarakkhitassa saddhivihārikabhūto upasampadāpekkho, “This Buddharakkhita, who is seeking the full ordination, is the student of Venerable Dhammarakkhita.” I have followed this interpretation, and thus my translation “with venerable so-and-so”.

pli-tv-kd1:76.10.4.pli: Itthannāmo saṅghaṁ upasampadaṁ yācati itthannāmena upajjhāyena.
pli-tv-kd1:76.10.4.eng: So-and-so is asking the Sangha for the full ordination with so-and-so as his preceptor.

pli-tv-kd1:76.10.5.pli: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmaṁ upasampādeyya itthannāmena upajjhāyena.
pli-tv-kd1:76.10.5.eng: If the Sangha is ready, it should give the full ordination to so-and-so with so-and-so as his preceptor.

pli-tv-kd1:76.11.1.eng: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd1:76.11.2.pli: Ayaṁ itthannāmo itthannāmassa āyasmato upasampadāpekkho, parisuddho antarāyikehi dhammehi, paripuṇṇassa pattacīvaraṁ.
pli-tv-kd1:76.11.2.eng: So-and-so is seeking the full ordination with venerable so-and-so. He is free from obstacles and his bowl and robes are complete.

pli-tv-kd1:76.11.3.pli: Itthannāmo saṅghaṁ upasampadaṁ yācati itthannāmena upajjhāyena.
pli-tv-kd1:76.11.3.eng: So-and-so is asking the Sangha for the full ordination with so-and-so as his preceptor.

pli-tv-kd1:76.11.4.eng: The Sangha gives the full ordination to so-and-so with so-and-so as his preceptor.

pli-tv-kd1:76.11.5.pli: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa upasampadā itthannāmena upajjhāyena, so tuṇhassa;
pli-tv-kd1:76.11.5.eng: Any monk who approves of giving the full ordination to so-and-so with so-and-so as his preceptor should remain silent.

pli-tv-kd1:76.11.6.eng: Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up.

pli-tv-kd1:76.12.1.eng: For the second time, I speak on this matter.

pli-tv-kd1:76.12.2.eng: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd1:76.12.3.pli: Ayaṁ itthannāmo itthannāmassa āyasmato upasampadāpekkho, parisuddho antarāyikehi dhammehi, paripuṇṇassa pattacīvaraṁ.
pli-tv-kd1:76.12.3.eng: So-and-so is seeking the full ordination with venerable so-and-so. He is free from obstacles and his bowl and robes are complete.

pli-tv-kd1:76.12.4.pli: Itthannāmo saṅghaṁ upasampadaṁ yācati itthannāmena upajjhāyena.
pli-tv-kd1:76.12.4.eng: So-and-so is asking the Sangha for the full ordination with so-and-so as his preceptor.

pli-tv-kd1:76.12.5.eng: The Sangha gives the full ordination to so-and-so with so-and-so as his preceptor.

pli-tv-kd1:76.12.6.pli: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa upasampadā itthannāmena upajjhāyena, so tuṇhassa;
pli-tv-kd1:76.12.6.eng: Any monk who approves of giving the full ordination to so-and-so with so-and-so as his preceptor should remain silent.

pli-tv-kd1:76.12.7.eng: Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up.

pli-tv-kd1:76.12.8.eng: For the third time, I speak on this matter.

pli-tv-kd1:76.12.9.eng: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd1:76.12.10.pli: Ayaṁ itthannāmo itthannāmassa āyasmato upasampadāpekkho, parisuddho antarāyikehi dhammehi, paripuṇṇassa pattacīvaraṁ.
pli-tv-kd1:76.12.10.eng: So-and-so is seeking the full ordination with venerable so-and-so. He is free from obstacles and his bowl and robes are complete.

pli-tv-kd1:76.12.11.pli: Itthannāmo saṅghaṁ upasampadaṁ yācati itthannāmena upajjhāyena.
pli-tv-kd1:76.12.11.eng: So-and-so is asking the Sangha for the full ordination with so-and-so as his preceptor.

pli-tv-kd1:76.12.12.eng: The Sangha gives the full ordination to so-and-so with so-and-so as his preceptor.

pli-tv-kd1:76.12.13.pli: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa upasampadā itthannāmena upajjhāyena, so tuṇhassa;
pli-tv-kd1:76.12.13.eng: Any monk who approves of giving the full ordination to so-and-so with so-and-so as his preceptor should remain silent.

pli-tv-kd1:76.12.14.eng: Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up.

pli-tv-kd1:76.12.15.eng: The Sangha has given the full ordination to so-and-so with so-and-so as his preceptor.

pli-tv-kd1:76.12.16.pli: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’”ti.
pli-tv-kd1:76.12.16.eng: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I’ll remember it thus.’”

pli-tv-kd1:76.12.17.eng: The procedure of full ordination is finished.

pli-tv-kd1:77.1.1.pli: “Tāvadeva chāyā metabbā, utuppamāṇaṁ ācikkhitabbaṁ, divasabhāgo ācikkhitabbo, saṅgīti ācikkhitabbā, cattāro nissayā ācikkhitabbā—
pli-tv-kd1:77.1.1.eng: “Straightaway the time should be noted and the date should be pointed out. These should be declared jointly to everyone. And the four supports should be pointed out to him:
pli-tv-kd1:77.1.1.comm: Sp 3.128: Chāyā metabbāti ekaporisā vā dviporisā vāti chāyā metabbā. Utuppamāṇaṁ ācikkhitabbanti “vassāno hemanto gimho”ti evaṁ utuppamāṇaṁ ācikkhitabbaṁ. Ettha ca utuyeva utuppamāṇaṁ. Sace vassānādayo aparipuṇṇā honti, yattakehi divasehi yassa yo utu aparipuṇṇo, te divase sallakkhetvā so divasabhāgo ācikkhitabbo. Atha vā “ayaṁ nāma utu, so ca kho paripuṇṇo vā aparipuṇṇo vā”ti evaṁ utuppamāṇaṁ ācikkhitabbaṁ. “Pubbaṇho vā sāyanho vā”ti evaṁ divasabhāgo ācikkhitabbo, “Chāyā metabbā: ‘It is the height of one man or the height of two men’, the length of the shade is to be measured. ‘The measure of the season (utuppamāṇa) should be pointed out’: ‘It is the rainy season, the cold season, the hot season’, in this way the measure of the season should be pointed out. In this case the measure of the season is just the season. If the rainy season, etc., is not complete, one should calculate the days until the completion of the season; that share of days (divasabhāga) is to be pointed out. Alternatively, ‘This is the name of the season, and it is complete or incomplete (by so many days)’, in this way the date is to be pointed out. ‘It is morning or evening’, in this way the part of the day is to be pointed out.” I have followed the latter of these two ways of understanding these terms, see the CPD. Saṅgīti ācikkhitabbā, literally, “A joint recitation is to be declared”, which is rather cryptic. Sp 3.128: Saṅgītīti idameva sabbaṁ ekato katvā “tvaṁ kiṁ labhasi, kā te chāyā, kiṁ utuppamāṇaṁ, ko divasabhāgo”ti puṭṭho “idaṁ nāma labhāmi – vassaṁ vā hemantaṁ vā gimhaṁ vā, ayaṁ me chāyā, idaṁ utuppamāṇaṁ, ayaṁ divasabhāgoti vadeyyāsī”ti evaṁ ācikkhitabbaṁ, “Saṅgīti: here it means: having brought everyone together, it should pointed out: “When you are asked, ‘What did you have? What time did you have? What date did you have?’ you should reply, ‘I had this: it was the rainy season/the cold season/the hot season; I had this time; I had this date.’” The point seems to be that a newly ordained monk should remember the time and date of his ordination so that he may respond to questions about it in future. Vmv 3.128 clarifies: Chāyādikameva sabbaṁ saṅgahetvā gāyitabbato kathetabbato saṅgītīti āha “idamevā”tiādi. Tattha ekato katvā ācikkhitabbaṁ. Tvaṁ kiṁ labhasīti tvaṁ upasampādanakāle kataravassaṁ, katarautuñca labhasi, katarasmiṁ te upasampadā laddhāti attho, “‘Here’, etc., means: having collected all—that is the time, etc.—saṅgīti is said because it is to be chanted, because it is to be declared. In regard to this, having brought (everyone) together, it is to be pointed out. ‘What did you have’ means: at the time of the ordination, which year did you have, which season; your ordination was obtained in which one?”

pli-tv-kd1:77.1.2.eng: ‘One gone forth is supported by almsfood.

pli-tv-kd1:77.1.3.eng: You should persevere with this for life.

pli-tv-kd1:77.1.5.pli: saṅghabhattaṁ, uddesabhattaṁ, nimantanaṁ, salākabhattaṁ, pakkhikaṁ, uposathikaṁ, pāṭipadikaṁ.
pli-tv-kd1:77.1.5.eng: a meal for the Sangha, a meal for designated monks, an invitational meal, a meal for which lots are drawn, a half-monthly meal, a meal on the observance day, and a meal on the day after the observance day.

pli-tv-kd1:77.1.6.eng: One gone forth is supported by rag-robes.

pli-tv-kd1:77.1.7.eng: You should persevere with this for life.

pli-tv-kd1:77.1.9.pli: khomaṁ, kappāsikaṁ, koseyyaṁ, kambalaṁ, sāṇaṁ, bhaṅgaṁ.
pli-tv-kd1:77.1.9.eng: linen, cotton, silk, wool, sunn hemp, and hemp.

pli-tv-kd1:77.1.10.eng: One gone forth is supported by the foot of a tree as a resting place.

pli-tv-kd1:77.1.11.eng: You should persevere with this for life.

pli-tv-kd1:77.1.13.eng: a dwelling, a stilt house, and a cave.
pli-tv-kd1:77.1.13.comm: For an explanation of the renderings “stilt house” and “cave” respectively for pāsāda and guhā, see Appendix of Technical Terms. Apart from the vihāra, “a dwelling”, and the guhā, “a cave”, the Pali mentions three kinds of buildings, the aḍḍhayoga, the pāsāda, and the hammiya, all of which, according to the commentaries, are different kinds of pāsāda, “stilt houses”. Rather than try to differentiate between these buildings, which is unlikely to be useful from a practical perspective, I have instead grouped them together as “stilt house”. Here is what the commentaries have to say. Sp 4.294: Aḍḍhayogoti supaṇṇavaṅkagehaṁ, “An aḍḍhayoga is a house bent like a supaṇṇa.” Sp-ṭ 4.294 clarifies: Supaṇṇavaṅkagehanti garuḷapakkhasaṇṭhānena katagehaṁ, “Supaṇṇavaṅkageha: a house made in the shape of the wings of a garuḷa.” A garuḷa, better known in its Sanskrit form garuḍa, is a mythological bird. Sp 4.294 continues: Pāsādoti dīghapāsādo. Hammiyanti upariākāsatale patiṭṭhitakūṭāgāro pāsādoyeva, “A pāsāda is a long stilt house. A hammiya is just a pāsāda that has an upper room on top of its flat roof.” At Sp-ṭ 3.74, however, we find slightly different explanations. It seems clear, however, that all three are stilt houses and that they are distinguished according to their shape and the kind of roof they possess.

pli-tv-kd1:77.1.14.eng: One gone forth is supported by medicine of fermented urine.

pli-tv-kd1:77.1.15.eng: You should persevere with this for life.

pli-tv-kd1:77.1.17.eng: ghee, butter, oil, honey, and syrup.’”

pli-tv-kd1:78.1.0.eng: 65. The four things not to be done

pli-tv-kd1:78.1.1.pli: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū aññataraṁ bhikkhuṁ upasampādetvā ekakaṁ ohāya pakkamiṁsu.
pli-tv-kd1:78.1.1.eng: On one occasion, the monks gave the full ordination to someone and then departed.

pli-tv-kd1:78.1.2.pli: So pacchā ekakova āgacchanto antarāmagge purāṇadutiyikāya samāgañchi.
pli-tv-kd1:78.1.2.eng: The newly ordained monk lagged behind, walking by himself. On the way he met his old wife.

pli-tv-kd1:78.1.6.eng: “It’s difficult for those gone forth to get sex.

pli-tv-kd1:78.1.8.pli: So tassā methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevitvā cirena agamāsi.

pli-tv-kd1:78.1.10.eng: When he caught up with the monks, they asked him what had taken him so long.

pli-tv-kd1:78.2.1.pli: Atha kho so bhikkhu bhikkhūnaṁ etamatthaṁ ārocesi.
pli-tv-kd1:78.2.1.eng: He told them what had happened,

pli-tv-kd1:78.2.3.pli: “Anujānāmi, bhikkhave, upasampādetvā dutiyaṁ dātuṁ, cattāri ca akaraṇīyāni ācikkhituṁ—
pli-tv-kd1:78.2.3.eng: “When you have given the full ordination to someone, you should give him a companion and point out the four things not to be done:

pli-tv-kd1:78.2.4.pli: Upasampannena bhikkhunā methuno dhammo na paṭisevitabbo, antamaso tiracchānagatāyapi.
pli-tv-kd1:78.2.4.eng: A monk who’s fully ordained shouldn’t have sexual intercourse, not even with an animal.

pli-tv-kd1:78.2.5.pli: Yo bhikkhu methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevati, assamaṇo hoti asakyaputtiyo.
pli-tv-kd1:78.2.5.eng: If he has sexual intercourse, he’s not an ascetic, not a Sakyan monastic.

pli-tv-kd1:78.2.6.pli: Seyyathāpi nāma puriso sīsacchinno abhabbo tena sarīrabandhanena jīvituṁ;
pli-tv-kd1:78.2.6.eng: Just as a man with his head cut off is unable to continue living by reconnecting it to the body,

pli-tv-kd1:78.2.7.pli: evameva bhikkhu methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevitvā assamaṇo hoti asakyaputtiyo.
pli-tv-kd1:78.2.7.eng: so too is a monk who has had sexual intercourse not an ascetic, not a Sakyan monastic.

pli-tv-kd1:78.2.8.eng: You shouldn’t do this for as long as you live.

pli-tv-kd1:78.3.1.pli: Upasampannena bhikkhunā adinnaṁ theyyasaṅkhātaṁ na ādātabbaṁ, antamaso tiṇasalākaṁ upādāya.
pli-tv-kd1:78.3.1.eng: A monk who’s fully ordained shouldn’t steal, not even a straw.

pli-tv-kd1:78.3.2.pli: Yo bhikkhu pādaṁ vā pādārahaṁ vā atirekapādaṁ vā adinnaṁ theyyasaṅkhātaṁ ādiyati, assamaṇo hoti asakyaputtiyo.
pli-tv-kd1:78.3.2.eng: If he steals a pāda coin, the value of a pāda, or more than a pāda, he’s not an ascetic, not a Sakyan monastic.

pli-tv-kd1:78.3.3.pli: Seyyathāpi nāma paṇḍupalāso bandhanā pavutto abhabbo haritatthāya;
pli-tv-kd1:78.3.3.eng: Just as a fallen, withered leaf is incapable of becoming green again,

pli-tv-kd1:78.3.4.pli: evameva bhikkhu pādaṁ vā pādārahaṁ vā atirekapādaṁ vā adinnaṁ theyyasaṅkhātaṁ ādiyitvā assamaṇo hoti asakyaputtiyo.
pli-tv-kd1:78.3.4.eng: so too is a monk who, intending to steal, takes an ungiven pāda coin, the value of a pāda, or more than a pāda not an ascetic, not a Sakyan monastic.

pli-tv-kd1:78.3.5.eng: You shouldn’t do this for as long as you live.

pli-tv-kd1:78.4.1.pli: Upasampannena bhikkhunā sañcicca pāṇo jīvitā na voropetabbo, antamaso kunthakipillikaṁ upādāya.
pli-tv-kd1:78.4.1.eng: A monk who’s fully ordained shouldn’t intentionally kill a living being, not even a small insect.

pli-tv-kd1:78.4.2.pli: Yo bhikkhu sañcicca manussaviggahaṁ jīvitā voropeti, antamaso gabbhapātanaṁ upādāya, assamaṇo hoti asakyaputtiyo.
pli-tv-kd1:78.4.2.eng: If he intentionally kills a human being, even causing an abortion, he’s not an ascetic, not a Sakyan monastic.

pli-tv-kd1:78.4.3.pli: Seyyathāpi nāma puthusilā dvedhā bhinnā appaṭisandhikā hoti;
pli-tv-kd1:78.4.3.eng: Just as an ordinary stone that has broken in half cannot be put back together again,

pli-tv-kd1:78.4.4.pli: evameva bhikkhu sañcicca manussaviggahaṁ jīvitā voropetvā assamaṇo hoti asakyaputtiyo.
pli-tv-kd1:78.4.4.eng: so too is a monk who has intentionally killed a human being not an ascetic, not a Sakyan monastic.

pli-tv-kd1:78.4.5.eng: You shouldn’t do this for as long as you live.

pli-tv-kd1:78.5.1.pli: Upasampannena bhikkhunā uttarimanussadhammo na ullapitabbo, antamaso ‘suññāgāre abhiramāmī’ti.
pli-tv-kd1:78.5.1.eng: A monk who’s fully ordained shouldn’t claim a superhuman quality, not even just saying, ‘I delight in solitude.’

pli-tv-kd1:78.5.2.pli: Yo bhikkhu pāpiccho icchāpakato asantaṁ abhūtaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ ullapati jhānaṁ vā vimokkhaṁ vā samādhiṁ vā samāpattiṁ vā maggaṁ vā phalaṁ vā, assamaṇo hoti asakyaputtiyo.
pli-tv-kd1:78.5.2.eng: If, because he has bad desires and is overcome by desire, he claims to have a non-existent superhuman quality—whether absorption, release, stillness, attainment, path, or fruit—he’s not an ascetic, not a Sakyan monastic.

pli-tv-kd1:78.5.3.pli: Seyyathāpi nāma tālo matthakacchinno abhabbo puna viruḷhiyā;
pli-tv-kd1:78.5.3.eng: Just as a palm tree with its crown cut off is incapable of further growth,

pli-tv-kd1:78.5.4.pli: evameva bhikkhu pāpiccho icchāpakato asantaṁ abhūtaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ ullapitvā assamaṇo hoti asakyaputtiyo.
pli-tv-kd1:78.5.4.eng: so too is a monk with bad desires, overcome by desire, who claims to have non-existent superhuman quality not an ascetic, not a Sakyan monastic.

pli-tv-kd1:78.5.5.eng: You shouldn’t do this for as long as you live.”

pli-tv-kd1:78.5.6.eng: The four things not to be done are finished.

pli-tv-kd1:79.1.0.eng: 66. The one ejected for not recognizing an offense

pli-tv-kd1:79.1.1.pli: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu āpattiyā adassane ukkhittako vibbhami.
pli-tv-kd1:79.1.1.eng: At one time a certain monk disrobed after being ejected for not recognizing an offense.

pli-tv-kd1:79.1.2.pli: So puna paccāgantvā bhikkhū upasampadaṁ yāci.
pli-tv-kd1:79.1.2.eng: He then returned and asked the monks for the full ordination.

pli-tv-kd1:79.1.4.pli: “Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu āpattiyā adassane ukkhittako vibbhamati.
pli-tv-kd1:79.1.4.eng: “When a monk disrobes after being ejected for not recognizing an offense,

pli-tv-kd1:79.1.5.pli: So puna paccāgantvā bhikkhū upasampadaṁ yācati.
pli-tv-kd1:79.1.5.eng: but then returns and asks the monks for the full ordination,

pli-tv-kd1:79.1.8.eng: If he says, ‘I will,’ he should be given the going forth.

pli-tv-kd1:79.1.9.eng: If he says, ‘I won’t,’ he should not.

pli-tv-kd1:79.1.10.eng: When he’s been given the going forth, he should be asked again,

pli-tv-kd1:79.1.12.eng: If he says, ‘I will,’ he should be given the full ordination.

pli-tv-kd1:79.1.13.eng: If he says, ‘I won’t,’ he should not.

pli-tv-kd1:79.2.1.eng: When he’s been given the full ordination, he should be asked again,

pli-tv-kd1:79.2.3.eng: If he says, ‘I will,’ he should be readmitted.

pli-tv-kd1:79.2.4.eng: If he says, ‘I won’t,’ he should not.

pli-tv-kd1:79.2.5.eng: When he’s been readmitted, he should be asked again,

pli-tv-kd1:79.2.7.eng: If he recognizes it, it’s good.

pli-tv-kd1:79.2.8.pli: No ce passati, labbhamānāya sāmaggiyā puna ukkhipitabbo.
pli-tv-kd1:79.2.8.eng: If he doesn’t recognize it and you’re unanimous, he should be ejected once more.

pli-tv-kd1:79.2.9.eng: If you’re not unanimous, there’s no offense in living with him or in doing formal meetings of the community together.
pli-tv-kd1:79.2.9.comm: See definitions of sambhoga and saṁvāsa at Bu Pc 69:2.1.14 and Bu Pc 69:2.1.21.

pli-tv-kd1:79.3.1.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhittako vibbhamati.
pli-tv-kd1:79.3.1.eng: “When a monk disrobes after being ejected for not making amends for an offense,

pli-tv-kd1:79.3.2.pli: So puna paccāgantvā bhikkhū upasampadaṁ yācati.
pli-tv-kd1:79.3.2.eng: but then returns and asks the monks for the full ordination,

pli-tv-kd1:79.3.4.eng: ‘Will you make amends for that offense?’

pli-tv-kd1:79.3.5.eng: If he says, ‘I will,’ he should be given the going forth.

pli-tv-kd1:79.3.6.eng: If he says, ‘I won’t,’ he should not.

pli-tv-kd1:79.3.7.eng: When he’s been given the going forth, he should be asked again,

pli-tv-kd1:79.3.8.eng: ‘Will you make amends for that offense?’

pli-tv-kd1:79.3.9.eng: If he says, ‘I will,’ he should be given the full ordination.

pli-tv-kd1:79.3.10.eng: If he says, ‘I won’t,’ he should not.

pli-tv-kd1:79.3.11.eng: When he’s been given the full ordination, he should be asked again,

pli-tv-kd1:79.3.12.eng: ‘Will you make amends for that offense?’

pli-tv-kd1:79.3.13.eng: If he says, ‘I will,’ he should be readmitted.

pli-tv-kd1:79.3.14.eng: If he says, ‘I won’t,’ he should not.

pli-tv-kd1:79.3.15.eng: When he’s been readmitted, he should be told,

pli-tv-kd1:79.3.18.pli: No ce paṭikaroti labbhamānāya sāmaggiyā puna ukkhipitabbo.
pli-tv-kd1:79.3.18.eng: If he doesn’t and you’re unanimous, he should be ejected once more.

pli-tv-kd1:79.3.19.eng: If you’re not unanimous, there’s no offense in living with him or in doing formal meetings of the community together.

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.1.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pāpikāya diṭṭhiyā appaṭinissagge ukkhittako vibbhamati.
pli-tv-kd1:79.4.1.eng: “When a monk disrobes after being ejected for not giving up a bad view,

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.2.pli: So puna paccāgantvā bhikkhū upasampadaṁ yācati.
pli-tv-kd1:79.4.2.eng: but then returns and asks the monks for the full ordination,

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.4.eng: ‘Will you give up that bad view?’

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.5.eng: If he says, ‘I will,’ he should be given the going forth.

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.6.eng: If he says, ‘I won’t,’ he should not.

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.7.eng: When he’s been given the going forth, he should be asked again,

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.8.eng: ‘Will you give up that bad view?’

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.9.eng: If he says, ‘I will,’ he should be given the full ordination.

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.10.eng: If he says, ‘I won’t,’ he should not.

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.11.eng: When he’s been given the full ordination, he should be asked again,

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.12.eng: ‘Will you give up that bad view?’

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.13.eng: If he says, ‘I will,’ he should be readmitted.

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.14.eng: If he says, ‘I won’t,’ he should not.

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.15.eng: When he’s been readmitted, he should be told,

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.18.pli: No ce paṭinissajjati, labbhamānāya sāmaggiyā puna ukkhipitabbo.
pli-tv-kd1:79.4.18.eng: If he doesn’t and you’re unanimous, he should be ejected once more.

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.19.eng: If you’re not unanimous, there’s no offense in living with him or in doing formal meetings of the community together.”

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.20.eng: The great chapter, the first, is finished.

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.21.comm: Vmv 3.131: Vinayamhītiādigāthāsu niggahānanti niggahakaraṇesu. Pāpiccheti pāpapuggalānaṁ niggahakaraṇesu, lajjīnaṁ paggahesu ca pesalānaṁ sukhāvahe mahante vinayamhi yathā atthakārī atthānuguṇaṁ karontova yasmā yoniso paṭipajjati nāma hoti, tasmā uddānaṁ pavakkhāmīti sambandhayojanā daṭṭhabbā; “In regard to the verses beginning with vinayamhi: niggahānaṁ means concerning the production of restraint. Pāpicche means in regard to the production of restraint of bad people and in regard to helping those who have a sense of conscience and those who are good, in the great Monastic Law which brings happiness, concerning one making and helping what is beneficial, wherefore it is called one practicing wisely, therefore I speak this summary. It is to be seen as connected together.”

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.22.comm: Vmv 3.131 reads mahante vinayamhi, “In the great Monastic Law”, which I follow.

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.23.eng: Which brings happiness to those who are good,

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.24.eng: Restrains those who have bad desires,
pli-tv-kd1:79.4.24.comm: Reading niggahe ca pāpicchānaṁ with the PTS edition.

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.25.eng: And helps those with a sense of conscience;
pli-tv-kd1:79.4.25.comm: I am not clear here on the function of the locative plural esu, but I am assuming the construction is parallel to the previous two lines.

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.26.eng: And which is for the upkeep of Buddhism,

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.29.eng: Which is safe, carefully laid down, without doubt—

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.30.eng: That is, the Chapters and the Monastic Law,

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.31.eng: The Compendium and the Key Terms—
pli-tv-kd1:79.4.31.comm: In this sort of context, and perhaps elsewhere too, Key Terms is usually a reference to the two Pātimokkhas. Sp 5.325: Pātimokkhanti dve mātikā na jānāti, “Pātimokkha: he does not know the two (collections of) Key Terms.”

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.32.eng: In this the skillful who does what’s beneficial,

pli-tv-kd1:79.4.48.comm: Asesetuṁ is presumably a denominative form of asesa, “without remainder”. See CPD, sv. asesita.
